description: Test corpus for the saas-security skill. Each fixture is a SaaS connector / multi-tenant data-access snippet; schema-driven means the runner smoke-validates shape while detect marks code violating a NEVER (hard-coded/shared SaaS token, missing tenant scoping, unverified webhook) and ignore marks code following the ALWAYS rules (secrets-manager tokens, tenant-scoped queries, verified webhook signatures).

fixtures.reason: Query is scoped only by row id, not tenant_id, so any authenticated tenant can read another tenant's invoice (cross-tenant IDOR). CWE-284 Improper Access Control.

A real-format Slack bot token (xoxb-) is hard-coded in source; such tokens are mass-scanned on public repos within minutes. Violates the NEVER 'Hard-code a SaaS API token'. CWE-798 Use of Hard-coded Credentials.

A single person-bound Atlassian token is shared across multiple integrations, inheriting the human's privileges and widening blast radius. Violates the NEVER 'Share a single personal API token across services'. CWE-284.
Query is constrained by both id and the authenticated tenant_id, so a tenant can only read its own rows. Enforces per-tenant isolation; no cross-tenant IDOR.
The Slack token is loaded from a secrets manager at runtime, never inlined in source or the image. Follows the ALWAYS 'Store SaaS API tokens in a secrets manager' rule.

Validates the HubSpot v3 HMAC signature and the timestamp window before trusting the request, using a constant-time compare and a secret from env. Follows the ALWAYS 'validate the signature and the timestamp window on every inbound request'.
